name: Coastal Shield
design_intent: RMOOZ authoring training; multi-side air campaign exercise

sides.doctrine: Offensive air campaign, precision strikes, force sustainment via tankers
Static defense, air superiority denial, facility protection

map.terrain: Coastal island state with inland plateaus
map.note: Fictional Meridian region (South Pacific analog). No real-world correspondence.

objectives.name: Meridian North Power Station
objectives.description: Strategic dual-use power generation facility, 8 years old. Regional infrastructure hub.

defenses.defense_description: Long-range SAM battalion, medium-range SAM coverage, AAA ring

constraints: Do NOT damage civilian power distribution grid

objectives.name: Meridian East Power Station
objectives.description: Coastal power generation facility, 5 years old. Port city infrastructure.

defenses.defense_description: Medium-range SAM coverage, AAA ring, coastal fighter base nearby

constraints: Minimize civilian casualties (coastal town 8 km away)
Collateral constraint: High population density nearby

objectives.name: Meridian Central Power Station
objectives.description: Inland power generation facility, 3 years old. Most heavily defended. Core infrastructure.

defenses.defense_description: Long-range + medium-range SAM layered defense, concentrated AAA, nearby fighter base

constraints: Do NOT damage civilian infrastructure within 15 km radius
objectives.value: Very High (central power hub, hardest defended)

objectives.name: Meridian South Power Station
objectives.description: Offshore/coastal power generation facility, 2 years old. Most remote. Lowest air defense density.

defenses.defense_description: Medium-range SAM coverage, AAA ring, farthest from fighter bases

constraints: Access constraint: Longer logistics routes for coalition

BLUE.name: Bretania Forward Air Base
BLUE.description: Coalition staging base in friendly territory. Primary strike staging.

BLUE.name: Diego-Analog Remote Base
BLUE.description: Ultra-remote coalition base. Strategic bombers and tanker staging only.

RED.name: Meridia North Air Base
RED.aircraft_based: F-4 Phantom II variants (older generation fighters)

RED.name: Meridia East Air Base
RED.aircraft_based: F-4 Phantom II variants (older) + F-5 Tiger II variants (older)

RED.name: Meridia Central Air Base
RED.aircraft_based: MiG-29 Fulcrum A (modern fighters, most dangerous)
RED.readiness: HIGH (some aircraft likely airborne, nearest to Central Power Station)

RED.name: Meridia Southwest Air Base
RED.aircraft_based: F-5 Tiger II variants (older generation fighters)

BLUE.name: Andor Air Superiority Squadron
BLUE.doctrine: Combat air patrol, intercept Meridian fighters, protect strike package
BLUE.capability_profile: High speed, long-range missiles, radar tracking

BLUE.name: Andor Strike Squadron
BLUE.doctrine: Primary strike against power stations; secondary air-to-air
BLUE.capability_profile: Medium range, precision air-to-ground, some air-to-air
BLUE.fuel_requirement: Heavy tanker support (long missions from distant bases)

BLUE.name: Bretania Strategic Bomber Wing
BLUE.doctrine: Strike most heavily-defended objectives (Central, possibly East)
BLUE.role: Heavy penetrating strike, hardened target destruction
BLUE.capability_profile: Strategic penetration, heavy payload, long range
BLUE.note: Limited quantity; high value targets only

BLUE.name: Andor EW Flight
BLUE.doctrine: Suppress Meridian air defense radars during strike

BLUE.name: Andor Early Warning Flight
BLUE.doctrine: Detect Meridian fighter launch, guide coalition air superiority fighters
BLUE.role: Air surveillance, fighter guidance, tactical assessment
BLUE.capability_profile: Air surveillance, fighter guidance, tactical assessment

BLUE.name: Coalition Tanker Flight
BLUE.doctrine: Enable long-range strike, sustain air superiority CAP, recover damaged aircraft
BLUE.capability_profile: Aerial refueling, large payload, continuous orbits
BLUE.constraint: Limited number; mission planning must account for refuel windows

BLUE.name: Andor CSAR Flight
BLUE.doctrine: Rescue downed pilots (if CSAR mode enabled)

RED.name: Meridian Fighter Corps (Older Generation)
RED.doctrine: Point defense (protect nearby facilities), limited range
RED.capability_profile: Medium range, older missiles (16-20 nm range), limited avionics
RED.note: F-4 Phantom II and F-5 Tiger II variants

RED.name: Meridian Fighter Corps (Modern)
RED.doctrine: Offensive CAP, attempt to intercept coalition strikes
RED.readiness: HIGH (some aircraft likely airborne during alert)
RED.capability_profile: Long-range missiles (35-50 nm), modern avionics, higher performance

RED.name: Meridian Long-Range SAM System
RED.distribution: Concentrated around North, East, Central facilities
RED.role: Air defense against bombers at distance
RED.capability_profile: Long-range SAM (S-300 analog), ~80 km effective range

RED.name: Meridian Medium-Range SAM System
RED.doctrine: Mid-layer defense, work with AAA for point defense
RED.role: Air defense against fighters and fighter-bombers
RED.capability_profile: Medium-range SAM (Patriot analog), ~30-40 km effective range

RED.name: Meridian Fixed AAA Batteries (Facility Defense)
RED.doctrine: Innermost defense ring, protect facility itself from low-altitude strikes

RED.name: Meridian Early Warning Radar Network
RED.distribution: Scattered across Meridian territory for overlapping coverage
RED.doctrine: Provide fighter-control center with tactical picture
RED.capability_profile: Long-range radar (~200+ km detection, ~100+ km tracking)

steps.title: H+0:00 — Pre-Strike Setup
steps.description: Coalition air campaign commander reviews situation, plans strike package.
player_decisions: Select which objectives to strike (1, 2, 3, or all 4 power stations)
Allocate aircraft by type to selected objectives
Plan tanker support orbit schedule and timing
Decide electronic warfare posture (full suppression vs. limited jamming)
Set rules of engagement (ROE) for collateral damage tolerance
events: Meridia begins fighter scramble from all bases
Meridian SAM system radars activated and powered up
Coalition early warning aircraft achieving orbit and initial surveillance
steps.victory_condition_check: Player selects objectives and commits forces

steps.title: H+0:20 — First Wave Strike Execution
steps.description: First wave of coalition aircraft departs bases and conducts initial strikes against selected targets.
expected_outcomes: Coalition aircraft losses: 2-6 (fighters from AAA, bombers from SAM)
Meridian fighter losses: 4-8 (to coalition air superiority)
Facilities struck: 1-2 (Meridian South likely success, North/East possible, Central difficult)
tactical_events: Air superiority fighters engage Meridian intercept attempts (multiple engagements)
Long-range bombers penetrate defenses against Central Power Station (high risk)
Fighter-bombers conduct precision strikes on lower-defended South facility
SAM systems engage coalition bombers (significant threat phase)
AAA defenses engage low-altitude fighter-bombers (expected attrition phase)
Coalition EW flights suppress Meridian radars for 8-12 minute windows
scoring_milestones: -5 points per friendly fighter lost (after first 5)
-15 points per strategic asset lost (tanker, EW, early warning)

steps.title: H+0:50 — Consolidation & Wave 2 Decision
steps.description: Coalition assesses first-wave damage, decides on continuation strategy.
player_decisions: Continue with planned second wave (higher risk, higher reward)?
Abort and consolidate damage achieved (lower risk, mission reduced scope)?
Shift targeting (strike East/Central instead of North if first wave losses high)?
Rearm and relaunch available fighter-bombers from forward base?
Launch second bomber wave or hold bombers in reserve?
steps.critical_decision_point: This decision point tests scenario design feasibility and player decision support
tactical_events_if_continue: Recover damaged aircraft to tanker orbits for emergency refueling
Rearm available fighter-bombers at bretania_forward (assume 15-20 min turnaround)
Launch second bomber wave if aircraft available and damage assessment supports targeting
Adjust fighter CAP posture based on Meridian air threat remaining
Expect increased Meridian SAM effectiveness (targets concentrated, defenses alert)
expected_cumulative_losses_if_continue: Coalition total losses: 6-12+ aircraft by end of Phase 3
Meridian total losses: 10-15+ fighters, SAM sites degraded

steps.title: H+1:30 — Battle Damage Assessment & Mission Complete
steps.description: Final scoring, outcome determination, after-action assessment.
scoring_determination: Count total coalition aircraft losses (final tally)
Determine victory level based on objectives achieved

victory_determination.strategic_victory: All 4 facilities damaged to 60%+, <5 friendly losses → 100 points
victory_determination.operational_victory: 3 of 4 facilities damaged to 60%+, <8 friendly losses → 75 points
victory_determination.tactical_victory: 2 of 4 facilities damaged to 60%+, <10 friendly losses → 50 points
victory_determination.failure: <2 facilities neutralized OR >10% civilian casualties → 0 points

post_game_analysis: Evaluate force readiness for potential Phase 2 (follow-up operations)
Assess feasibility of extended campaign if required by higher command
Review collateral damage impact on civilian infrastructure
Record lessons learned for next scenario iteration

strategic_victory.description: Neutralize all 4 power stations to 60%+ damage
strategic_victory.constraints: With <5 friendly aircraft lost AND civilian casualties <5%
strategic_victory.scenario_outcome: Coalition decisively degrades Meridian strategic power infrastructure

operational_victory.description: Neutralize 3 of 4 power stations to 60%+ damage
operational_victory.constraints: With <8 friendly aircraft lost AND civilian casualties <8%
operational_victory.scenario_outcome: Coalition successfully strikes majority of objectives; limited air losses

tactical_victory.description: Neutralize 2 of 4 power stations to 60%+ damage
tactical_victory.scenario_outcome: Coalition achieves partial success; significant air losses reduce mission effectiveness

failure.description: Neutralize <2 power stations OR inflict >10% civilian casualties
failure.constraints: Any outcome not meeting tactical victory threshold
failure.scenario_outcome: Mission fails; insufficient force or excessive collateral damage

BLUE_coalition.assumption_air_superiority: Andor coalition has air superiority fighters capable of longer-range engagement
BLUE_coalition.assumption_tanker_support: Tanker flights enable long-range operations; loss of tankers severely degrades capability
BLUE_coalition.assumption_early_warning: Early warning aircraft provide fighter control and tactical picture for intercepting Meridian fighters
BLUE_coalition.assumption_logistics: Supply, ammunition, and fuel logistics pre-positioned; assume unlimited munitions for scenario duration
BLUE_coalition.assumption_coordination: Coalition coordination between air superiority, strike, and support flights is assumed seamless

RED_meridia.assumption_alert_status: Meridian alert status RAISED due to detected coalition buildup
RED_meridia.assumption_fighter_scramble: Meridian fighters can scramble within 5-10 minutes of coalition approach detection
RED_meridia.assumption_sam_readiness: SAM systems manned and ready; not in active fire mode initially but can engage on radar lock
RED_meridia.assumption_radar_coverage: Early warning radars provide overlapping coverage of Meridian airspace; fighter-control center active
RED_meridia.assumption_air_defense_doctrine: Meridian will NOT launch preemptive strike; defensive posture only

design_notes.learning_intent: This scenario teaches RMOOZ scenario authoring patterns: multi-side conflict, defended objectives, readiness constraints, phased timeline, decision support.
design_notes.scope_simplification: Designed as viable RMOOZ pilot; reduced from full CMO scenario (fewer aircraft, consolidated SAM sites, shorter timeline).
design_notes.geographic_abstraction: Fictional Meridian region used to avoid real-world operational specificity. Scenario teaches structure, not targeting.
design_notes.mechanical_testing: Tests RMOOZ capabilities: 2-side conflict (not yet in public demos), objective damage mechanics, loss penalties, phase-based timeline.
design_notes.extensibility: Can extend to: 3-side conflict (add neutral civilian side), multi-day campaign (add Phase 5+), carrier operations (add naval units), dynamic weather (currently static).

metadata.source_inspiration: Iran Strike 2022 (CMO Community Scenario Pack 51) — abstracted to fictional Meridian region
metadata.rmooz_compatibility: Draft (requires testing in RMOOZ edit mode; steps 1-4 implemented; briefing text TBD)
metadata.next_steps: Import to RMOOZ edit mode, validate step structure, test objective mechanics, verify victory condition evaluation
